Title: Verify Predefined Products component appears in Builder.io insert menu
Preconditions:
1. Builder.io account with access to VCST QA space
2. QA environment with artifact deployed (vc-theme-b2b-vue-2.41.0-pr-2165-b9ab)
3. Logged into Builder.io: https://builder.io/content
Steps:
1. Navigate to Builder.io content editor
2. Create new page or open existing page
3. Click "+" or "Insert" button to add new component
4. Search or scroll for "Predefined Products" component
5. Verify component icon and description
Expected Result: Predefined Products component is visible and available for insertion with proper name and icon

Title: Insert Predefined Products component into page
Preconditions:
1. TC_VCST4553_001 passed
2. Builder.io page editor open
Steps:
1. Click on "Predefined Products" component in insert menu
2. Drag component to page canvas
3. Verify component preview renders
4. Verify component is selected
Expected Result: Component successfully inserted into page with configuration panel visible

Title: Verify all component configuration fields are present
Preconditions:
1. TC_VCST4553_002 passed
2. Predefined Products component inserted
3. Component settings panel open
Steps:
1. Verify "Title" text field exists
2. Verify "Subtitle" text field exists
3. Verify "Card Type" dropdown exists with options: full, short
4. Verify "Columns (Tablet)" field exists (accepts 2 or 3)
5. Verify "Columns (Desktop)" field exists (accepts 3 or 4)
6. Verify "SKU List" field exists (allows multiple entries)
7. Verify field labels and placeholders are clear
Expected Result: All 6 configuration fields present and functional: Title, Subtitle, Card Type, Columns (Tablet), Columns (Desktop), SKU List

Title: Verify component default values
Preconditions: 1. Predefined Products component inserted (fresh)
Steps:
1. Insert new Predefined Products component
2. Check Title field (empty or placeholder)
3. Check Subtitle field (empty or placeholder)
4. Check Card Type (default: full)
5. Check Columns (Tablet) (default: 2 or 3)
6. Check Columns (Desktop) (default: 3 or 4)
7. Check SKU List (empty)
Expected Result: All fields have sensible default values

Title: Add single valid SKU
Preconditions:
1. Predefined Products component inserted
2. Valid test SKU: SKU-001 (exists in QA catalog)
Steps:
1. Click "Add Item" or "+" in SKU List field
2. Enter valid SKU: SKU-001
3. Click outside field or press Enter
4. Verify SKU appears in list
5. Preview component in Builder.io editor
Expected Result: Single SKU successfully added and product preview displays

Title: Add multiple valid SKUs (3 SKUs)
Preconditions:
1. Predefined Products component inserted
2. Valid test SKUs: SKU-001, SKU-002, SKU-003
Steps:
1. Add first SKU: SKU-001
2. Add second SKU: SKU-002
3. Add third SKU: SKU-003
4. Verify all 3 SKUs visible in list
5. Preview component
Expected Result: All 3 SKUs successfully added and products preview correctly

Title: Add maximum SKUs (12 SKUs)
Preconditions:
1. Predefined Products component inserted
2. 12 valid test SKUs: SKU-001 through SKU-012
Steps:
1. Add SKUs 1-12 sequentially
2. Verify count shows 12 SKUs
3. Preview component in Builder.io
4. Verify no error or warning appears
Expected Result: Maximum 12 SKUs added successfully, preview shows all products, no validation alerts

Title: Attempt to add 13th SKU (exceed limit)
Preconditions: 1. Predefined Products component with 12 SKUs already added
Steps:
1. Verify 12 SKUs already in list
2. Attempt to add 13th SKU: SKU-013
3. Verify validation alert appears
4. Verify alert message indicates maximum 12 SKUs allowed
5. Verify 13th SKU not added
Expected Result: Validation prevents adding 13th SKU and displays clear error message. SKU list still shows only 12 SKUs.

Title: Add invalid SKU (non-existent product)
Preconditions:
1. Predefined Products component inserted
2. Invalid SKU: NON-EXISTENT-SKU-999
Steps:
1. Add invalid SKU: NON-EXISTENT-SKU-999
2. Save and preview in Builder.io
3. Publish page
4. View page on frontend
5. Verify other valid SKUs still display
Expected Result: Invalid SKU handled gracefully (skipped or placeholder shown). Page doesn't crash, other products display correctly.

Title: Add duplicate SKUs
Preconditions: 1. Predefined Products component inserted
Steps:
1. Add SKU: SKU-001
2. Attempt to add same SKU: SKU-001 again
3. Preview in Builder.io
4. Publish and view on frontend
Expected Result: Duplicate SKUs handled gracefully (prevented with warning OR de-duplicated). Product appears once on frontend.

Title: Remove SKU from list
Preconditions: 1. Predefined Products component with 3 SKUs: SKU-001, SKU-002, SKU-003
Steps:
1. Verify 3 SKUs in list
2. Click "Remove" or "X" button on SKU-002
3. Verify remaining SKUs: SKU-001, SKU-003
4. Preview component
Expected Result: SKU successfully removed. Only 2 products display (SKU-001, SKU-003).

Title: Clear all SKUs (empty list)
Preconditions: 1. Predefined Products component with 5 SKUs
Steps:
1. Remove all SKUs one by one
2. Verify SKU list is empty (0 SKUs)
3. Preview component in Builder.io
4. Publish and view on frontend
Expected Result: Empty SKU list handled gracefully (component hidden OR shows "No products" message)

Title: Reorder SKUs in Builder.io
Preconditions: 1. Predefined Products component with 4 SKUs in order: SKU-001, SKU-002, SKU-003, SKU-004
Steps:
1. Verify initial order in Builder.io: 001, 002, 003, 004
2. Drag SKU-003 to first position (or use arrows)
3. Drag SKU-004 to third position
4. Verify new order: 003, 001, 004, 002
5. Preview in Builder.io editor
6. Save and publish page
7. View page on frontend
Expected Result: Frontend displays products in exact reordered sequence: SKU-003, SKU-001, SKU-004, SKU-002

Title: Verify product order with 12 SKUs
Preconditions: 1. Predefined Products component with 12 SKUs in specific order (reverse order)
Steps:
1. Add 12 SKUs in reverse order (012 first, 001 last)
2. Preview in Builder.io
3. Publish page
4. View on frontend
Expected Result: All 12 products display in exact reverse order as specified

Title: Configure Title and Subtitle
Preconditions: 1. Predefined Products component with 3 SKUs
Steps:
1. Enter Title: "Featured Campaign Products"
2. Enter Subtitle: "Exclusive deals for February 2026"
3. Preview in Builder.io
4. Publish page
5. View on frontend
6. Verify subtitle position
7. Verify text styling
Expected Result: Title and subtitle display correctly on frontend above product list with proper styling

Title: Test without Title and Subtitle (optional fields)
Preconditions:
1. Predefined Products component with 3 SKUs
2. Title and Subtitle fields left empty
Steps:
1. Leave Title field empty
2. Leave Subtitle field empty
3. Preview in Builder.io
4. Publish and view on frontend
Expected Result: Component displays products without empty title/subtitle containers. No empty space shown.

Title: Configure Card Type - Full
Preconditions:
1. Predefined Products component with 4 SKUs
2. Card Type set to full
Steps:
1. Set Card Type to "full"
2. Preview in Builder.io
3. Publish and view on frontend
4. Verify card includes: Image, Name, Price, Description, Add to Cart button
Expected Result: Products display in full card format with complete information

Title: Configure Card Type - Short
Preconditions:
1. Predefined Products component with 4 SKUs
2. Card Type set to short
Steps:
1. Set Card Type to "short"
2. Preview in Builder.io
3. Publish and view on frontend
4. Verify card includes: Image, Name, Price (minimal)
5. Verify card excludes: Description, Add to Cart (or simplified)
Expected Result: Products display in short card format with minimal information

Title: Configure Tablet columns (2 columns)
Preconditions:
1. Predefined Products component with 6 SKUs
2. Tablet columns set to 2
Steps:
1. Set Columns (Tablet) to 2
2. Publish page
3. Open on tablet device (iPad) OR resize browser to 768px-1024px
4. Verify product grid layout
5. Scroll to view all products
Expected Result: Products display in 2-column grid on tablet viewport. 6 products in 3 rows (2 per row).

Title: Configure Tablet columns (3 columns)
Preconditions:
1. Predefined Products component with 6 SKUs
2. Tablet columns set to 3
Steps:
1. Set Columns (Tablet) to 3
2. Publish page
3. Open on tablet device OR resize browser to 768px-1024px
4. Verify product grid layout
5. Scroll to view all products
Expected Result: Products display in 3-column grid on tablet viewport. 6 products in 2 rows (3 per row).

Title: Configure Desktop columns (3 columns)
Preconditions:
1. Predefined Products component with 9 SKUs
2. Desktop columns set to 3
Steps:
1. Set Columns (Desktop) to 3
2. Publish page
3. Open page on desktop browser (1280px+ width)
4. Verify product grid layout
5. Verify all 9 products visible
Expected Result: Products display in 3-column grid on desktop. 9 products in 3 rows (3 per row).

Title: Configure Desktop columns (4 columns)
Preconditions:
1. Predefined Products component with 8 SKUs
2. Desktop columns set to 4
Steps:
1. Set Columns (Desktop) to 4
2. Publish page
3. Open page on desktop browser (1280px+ width)
4. Verify product grid layout
5. Verify all 8 products visible
Expected Result: Products display in 4-column grid on desktop. 8 products in 2 rows (4 per row).

Title: Verify frontend product display (complete workflow)
Preconditions:
1. Builder.io access
2. QA environment
Steps:
1. Create new page in Builder.io: "Test Campaign Page"
2. Insert Predefined Products component
3. Configure Title: "February Highlights"
4. Configure Subtitle: "Top picks for this month"
5. Add 5 SKUs: SKU-001 through SKU-005
6. Set Card Type: full
7. Set Tablet columns: 2
8. Set Desktop columns: 4
9. Preview in Builder.io
10. Publish page
11. Navigate to frontend URL
12. Verify title, subtitle, 5 products in order
13. Verify full card format
14. Resize to tablet (2 columns)
15. Resize to desktop (4 columns)
Expected Result: Complete workflow successful. Frontend displays exactly as configured: title, subtitle, 5 products in order, full cards, responsive columns.

Title: Verify product card clickability (navigation to PDP)
Preconditions:
1. Page with Predefined Products component published
2. 3 SKUs configured
Steps:
1. Navigate to page on frontend
2. Hover over first product card (SKU-001)
3. Click on product card or product name
4. Verify PDP loads correctly for SKU-001
5. Navigate back to campaign page
6. Click "Add to Cart" button on second product
Expected Result: Product cards are interactive. Clicking navigates to correct PDP. Add to Cart functional.

Title: Verify product information accuracy
Preconditions:
1. Page with Predefined Products component published
2. Known product: SKU-001 with specific name, price, image
Steps:
1. Navigate to page on frontend
2. Locate SKU-001 product card
3. Verify product name matches catalog
4. Verify product price matches catalog
5. Verify product image matches catalog
6. Verify product availability status
Expected Result: All product information matches catalog data exactly (name, price, image, status)

Title: Verify multiple Predefined Product List blocks on same page
Preconditions: 1. Builder.io page editor
Steps:
1. Insert first Predefined Products block
2. Configure Block 1: Title "Campaign A", 3 SKUs (001, 002, 003)
3. Insert second block below Block 1
4. Configure Block 2: Title "Campaign B", 4 SKUs (004, 005, 006, 007)
5. Preview in Builder.io
6. Publish page
7. View on frontend
8. Verify Block 1: "Campaign A" with 3 products
9. Verify Block 2: "Campaign B" with 4 products
10. Verify blocks are independent
Expected Result: Multiple blocks coexist independently on same page. Both display correctly without data mixing.

Title: Verify mobile viewport display (iPhone Safari)
Preconditions:
1. Page with Predefined Products component published
2. 6 SKUs configured
Steps:
1. Open page on iPhone Safari OR mobile viewport (375px-414px)
2. Verify products display in single column
3. Verify title and subtitle readable
4. Scroll through all products
5. Verify product cards fit viewport width (no horizontal scroll)
6. Tap on product card
7. Verify images load correctly
Expected Result: Mobile viewport displays products in single column with proper touch targets. No horizontal scroll. Touch interaction works.

Title: Product out of stock in predefined list
Preconditions: 1. Predefined Products component with 3 SKUs: SKU-001 (in stock), SKU-OUT-OF-STOCK, SKU-003 (in stock)
Steps:
1. Configure component with 3 SKUs including out-of-stock SKU
2. Publish and view on frontend
3. Verify all 3 products display
4. Verify out-of-stock product shows "Out of Stock" label
5. Verify "Add to Cart" button disabled/hidden for out-of-stock
6. Verify in-stock products have functional "Add to Cart"
Expected Result: Out-of-stock products display with clear status and disabled actions. Other products unaffected.

Title: Product unpublished or deleted after configuration
Preconditions:
1. Predefined Products component with 3 SKUs configured and published
2. One product "SKU-TEMP" then unpublished/deleted
Steps:
1. Configure with 3 SKUs including "SKU-TEMP"
2. Publish page (all 3 products display)
3. Unpublish or delete "SKU-TEMP" from Admin catalog
4. Reload frontend page (clear cache)
5. Verify unpublished product either hidden OR shows placeholder
6. Verify other 2 products still display correctly
Expected Result: Unpublished products hidden or show placeholder. Page doesn't crash. Other products unaffected.

Title: Save component without adding any SKUs
Preconditions:
1. Predefined Products component inserted
2. No SKUs added (empty)
Steps:
1. Insert Predefined Products component
2. Leave SKU list empty (0 SKUs)
3. Optionally set Title: "Coming Soon"
4. Publish page
5. View on frontend
6. Verify component either hidden OR shows empty state message
Expected Result: Empty component handled gracefully (hidden or empty state message). Page doesn't crash.

Title: Special characters in Title/Subtitle
Preconditions: 1. Predefined Products component with 2 SKUs
Steps:
1. Enter Title: "50% OFF! Save $100+ on Featured Items™"
2. Enter Subtitle: "Limited time <offer> & exclusive deals"
3. Preview in Builder.io
4. Publish and view on frontend
5. Verify no XSS vulnerability (HTML tags escaped)
Expected Result: Special characters escaped and display safely. No XSS vulnerability.

Title: Long product name/title overflow
Preconditions:
1. Predefined Products component with product having very long name
2. SKU-LONG-NAME
Steps:
1. Add SKU with very long name to component
2. Publish and view on frontend
3. Verify product name handling (truncated or wrapped)
4. Verify product card layout not broken
5. Verify other products unaffected
Expected Result: Long product names handled gracefully (truncation with ellipsis OR wrapped). Layout not broken.

Title: Cross-browser compatibility (Chrome Firefox Safari Edge)
Preconditions: 1. Page with Predefined Products component published (6 SKUs)
Steps:
1. Open page in Chrome (verify layout, styling, images)
2. Open page in Firefox (verify layout, styling, images)
3. Open page in Safari macOS (verify layout, styling, images)
4. Open page in Edge (verify layout, styling, images)
5. Compare rendering across browsers
Expected Result: Consistent display and functionality across all tested browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge)

Title: Keyboard navigation accessibility
Preconditions: 1. Page with Predefined Products component published (4 products)
Steps:
1. Navigate to page
2. Use Tab key to navigate to first product card
3. Press Tab to move through products (verify focus indicators)
4. Press Enter on focused product (navigate to PDP)
5. Return, Tab to "Add to Cart" button
6. Press Enter on button (product added)
7. Verify focus order is logical (top to bottom, left to right)
Expected Result: All interactive elements keyboard accessible with visible focus indicators. Focus order logical.

Title: Screen reader compatibility
Preconditions:
1. Page with Predefined Products component published
2. Screen reader enabled (NVDA, JAWS, or VoiceOver)
Steps:
1. Navigate to page with screen reader
2. Navigate to Predefined Products section
3. Navigate through product cards
4. Verify images have alt text
5. Verify links/buttons have descriptive labels
6. Verify product list structure is semantic
Expected Result: All content and functionality accessible via screen reader. Alt text present, labels clear, structure semantic.
